title: Coral DNA Extraction - Modified DNeasy PowerSoil Pro Kit
author: Luigi Colin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bww6pfhe

steps.instruction: Gently crush a small piece of coral in a mortar and pestle (~ 100 mg , in order not to overload the column though). And transfer in a PowerBead Pro Tube (or 2 ml Microcentrifuge). Note: For improved yield focus on adding more tissue and less carbonate from the skeleton, a scalpel
Add 800 µL of buffer CD1 and vortex briefly.
Add 83 µL of Proteinase K (2 ml) Qiagen Catalog #19131 and vortex briefly. Note: quality of Proteinase K can be tweaked depending on the quality and type of samples
Add 200 µL buffer CD2 and vortex for 00:00:05
Microfuge a 16000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .
Transfer supernatant to new tube (Max 700 µL ). Expect ~ 650 µL
Add 600 µL of buffer CD3, vortex for 00:00:05
Load 650 µL of the lysate onto an MB Spin Column and centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
Discard the flow-through and repeat step 9 to ensure that all the lysate has passed through the MB Spin Column.
Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a clean 2 ml Collection Tube. Avoid splashing any flow-through onto the MB Spin Column.
Add 500 µL of Solution EA to the MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
Discard the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column back into the same 2 ml Collection Tube.
Add 500 µL of Solution C5 to the MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
Discard the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column into a new 2 ml Collection
Centrifuge at up to 16000 x g, Room temperature, 3 minutes . Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a new 1.5ml Elution Tube.
Add 50 µL – 100 µL of Solution C6 to the centre of the white filter membrane. Note: To improve the yield use the lower volume and add a 00:10:00 incubation at room temperature
Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute . Discard the MB Spin Column. The DNA is now ready for downstream applications.

Incubate at 56 degrees for 60 minutes. Vortex for 00:00:10 every 00:15:00 (Ideally, do this on a shaking heat block, with shaking on full) Note: If on orbital incubator at a minimum of 300rpm or more, can skip the vortexing. Add an extra hour of incubation to increase yield. More than 02:00:00 will results in shearing of the longer DNA fragments
